meta.title: AgentFleet — Run coding agents across every machine you own
meta.description: AgentFleet turns your own devices into a distributed coding-agent fleet. Run prompts in parallel through OneDrive sync with no server or control plane.
meta.ogDescription: Fan out coding-agent tasks across laptops, desktops, and lab machines. No server, no SSH mesh, no control plane — coordinated through OneDrive.
landing.hook: Run one prompt across every device you own.
landing.subHook: AgentFleet turns laptops, desktops, and lab machines into a parallel coding-agent fabric using OneDrive as the coordination layer — no server, queue, SSH mesh, or control plane.
landing.valuePropZeroServersDesc: No backend, scheduler, database, queue, or inbound port. If OneDrive sync works, the fleet can coordinate.
landing.valuePropZeroConfigDesc: Every node runs its own local agent command and writes hostname-scoped results, so you can compare outputs side by side.
landing.valuePropEnterpriseDesc: Tasks and results stay in your Microsoft 365 / OneDrive environment. The web app calls Microsoft Graph directly.
landing.architectureTitle: One shared folder, many independent agents
landing.architectureDesc: Submit a task once. Each running machine sees the immutable task file, executes locally, and writes back a separate result file.
home.agentHint: Leave empty to use each node's default agent configuration.
home.nodesEmpty: No nodes have executed tasks yet. Run npx -y @daomar/agentfleet run on a machine to enroll it.
home.recentTasksEmpty: No tasks yet. Use the form above to submit your first task.
home.recentTasksFailed: Failed to load tasks. Please try again.
home.loadFailed: Failed to load task details. Please try again.
home.welcomeText: No AgentFleet workspace found in your OneDrive. Get started by installing AgentFleet on your first machine:
home.welcomeExplanation: This will create the AgentFleet workspace in your OneDrive and start watching for tasks.
home.welcomeHint: 💡 Make sure you signed in with the same Microsoft account used by your machines' OneDrive.
taskList.title: All Tasks
taskList.empty: No tasks found. Submit a task from the Home page.
taskList.loadFailed: Failed to load tasks. Please try again.
taskDetail.noResults: No results yet. Machines may still be processing this task.
settings.title: Settings
settings.agentDesc: Set the default agent command used when submitting tasks from this dashboard. Each node can override this with its own local configuration.
settings.aboutDesc: AgentFleet Web Dashboard connects to your OneDrive via Microsoft Graph API to manage tasks and monitor nodes controlled by the AgentFleet CLI.
settings.aboutSecurity: AgentFleet requires no tunnels, no exposed ports, and moves no data. All coordination happens through your existing OneDrive sync — nothing additional to audit, secure, or maintain.
landing.eyebrow: For developers running agents on more than one machine
landing.quickStartHint: Start this on each machine, then submit once from CLI or browser.
landing.diagram:
Machine A          Machine B          Machine C
agentfleet run     agentfleet run     agentfleet submit
     │                  │                   │
     └──────────┬───────┴──────────┬────────┘
                ▼                  ▼
        OneDrive / AgentFleet shared workspace
           tasks/  → immutable task JSON
           output/ → per-machine results
landing.examplesTitle: Try it on real agent work
landing.exampleReviewDesc: Ask every machine to inspect the same repository for security, reliability, or architecture risks.
landing.exampleRefactorDesc: Let separate machines attempt the same migration on separate clones, then compare patch quality.
landing.exampleBenchmarkDesc: Run one prompt across different local agents, models, branches, or hardware profiles.
